_meta.note: Supplement for SemEval techniques. PropaInsight models propaganda as techniques + appeals + intent.

commonConfusions: Not all expert citations are propaganda; distinguish authoritative sourcing from name-dropping
appeal-to-authority.propaInsightNote: Often overlaps with glittering generalities when the authority is idealized.

commonConfusions: Legitimate warnings vs fear-mongering; proportional vs disproportionate threat framing
appeal-to-fear-prejudice.propaInsightNote: Strong emotional arousal; often combined with loaded language.

commonConfusions: Bandwagon: genuine popularity vs fabricated consensus. Reductio: valid analogy vs inappropriate Nazi comparison.
bandwagon-reductio-ad-hitlerum.propaInsightNote: Two distinct sub-techniques; Reductio ad Hitlerum is a subtype of smears.

commonConfusions: Genuine dilemmas vs false dichotomy; 'either X or Y' when Z exists
black-and-white-fallacy.propaInsightNote: False dilemma; closes off middle-ground alternatives.

commonConfusions: Single cause vs multi-factor; post hoc fallacies
causal-oversimplification.propaInsightNote: Reduces complex causality to one factor.

commonConfusions: Legitimate skepticism vs manufactured doubt (e.g. climate denial playbook)
doubt.propaInsightNote: Questioning without evidence to sow confusion.

commonConfusions: Exaggeration vs hyperbole; minimisation vs proportional response
exaggeration-minimisation.propaInsightNote: Bidirectional: inflate enemy/risk or shrink own responsibility.

commonConfusions: Genuine patriotism vs instrumental use of symbols
flag-waving.propaInsightNote: Often with slogans; appeals to in-group pride.

commonConfusions: Emotive language vs loaded language (systematic slant); opinion vs manipulation
loaded-language.propaInsightNote: Core technique; vocabulary as persuasion tool.

commonConfusions: Name-calling vs legitimate criticism; labeling vs accurate description
name-calling-labeling.propaInsightNote: Overlaps with smears; negative epithets and stereotypes.

commonConfusions: Rhetorical emphasis vs manipulative repetition; mantra vs propaganda loop
repetition.propaInsightNote: Repetition increases perceived truth (illusory truth effect).

commonConfusions: Slogan vs headline; memorable phrase vs propaganda slogan
slogans.propaInsightNote: Short, punchy; often combined with flag-waving and repetition.

commonConfusions: Wise aphorism vs thought-terminator; common sense vs cliché
thought-terminating-cliches.propaInsightNote: Phrases that end reflection; 'it is what it is', 'common sense'.

commonConfusions: Whataboutism vs relevant comparison; straw man vs fair paraphrase; red herring vs tangent
whataboutism-straw-men-red-herring.propaInsightNote: Three related techniques: deflection, misrepresentation, distraction.
